subject: First Aid & Safety
title: The First Civilian First Aid Classes
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The First Civilian First Aid Classes.

hook.text: In 1878, a British army surgeon taught the first first aid class—to civilians, not soldiers.

buildup.text: Surgeon-Major Peter Shepherd partnered with the St John Ambulance Association.

discovery.text: The classes taught bandaging, splinting, and patient transport to factory workers and miners.

twist.text: Industrial accidents were killing thousands yearly—nobody had trained workers to respond.

climax.text: Within a decade, first aid training spread across the British Empire.

punchline.text: A military skill became a civilian right—and a lifesaver.

quiz.question: Who organized the first civilian first aid classes?
